title: Comment from Snyder, Brian, HHS-ONC-2025-0005, HHS-ONC-2025-0005-0001, 2025-23896
comment_text:
I submit my comment to this proposal as an attorney who frequently deals with audit trails. As those involved are aware, the purpose of the audit trail is to ensure integrity of the record. We cannot have a system where the patient or the provider cannot be sure the records are correct and accurate. With the current system, the audit trail tracks who accessed records, when he/she accessed them, and what was done to the record. If those safeguard are taken away, we will be left unaware if the records can be trusted. This is just as important to the healthcare provider as it is to the patient. For the provider, if he or she is sued without good reason, the provider should be able to rely on the records to prove his/her defense. For the patient, it is crucial to know if the records are reliable or if they have been manipulated. 

The other important element to this is that audit trails are critical in the fight to prevent healthcare fraud. Without knowing if records were tampered with, there will be no ability to determine if the charges/treatment were appropriate. 

We need systems in place to keep people honest. Without regulated audit trails, healthcare will become the wild west. This cannot happen, as it endangers patients and will exponentially increase healthcare fraud, which affects every taxpayer in our country. 

Brian Snyder
Attorney
